description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with dark, weathered shingles and visible patches, giving it a rustic, medieval appearance. The exterior walls are a mix of exposed timber framing and stonework, with intricate wooden crossbeams and textured stone at the base. Blue diamond-paned windows are set in both the upper and lower stories, some with decorative wooden trim, and the windows are arranged in an asymmetrical pattern. A prominent stone chimney rises along one side, and the front facade includes a small arched entryway with stone steps leading up to it. The roof is adorned with dormer windows and has visible signs of wear, such as missing shingles and patches. Overall, the structure exudes a historic, storybook charm reminiscent of European Tudor or medieval cottages.
description: A rustic, medieval-style house with a steep gable roof, exposed timber framing, stone base, and blue diamond-paned windows arranged asymmetrically.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, arched stone steps, central gable, and prominent blue windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tall stone chimney, roof patches, and a single dormer window.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying symmetrical timber framing, two blue windows, and the full height of the structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two dormer windows, visible roof wear, and stonework extending up the side.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with dormers and diamond-paned glass
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone entry with steps, small overhang, and decorative wooden trim
